content.cn_text: 第四部分。你将听到一段环境研究
content.en_text: Section 4 You will hear part of an environmental

content.cn_text: 讲座，内容是关于把昆虫作为食物来源的可能性。
content.en_text: studies lecture about the possibility of using insects as a source of food.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间查看第8页的第31至40题。
content.en_text: First you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40 on page 8.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第31至40题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 早上好。
content.en_text: Good morning.

content.cn_text: 过去几周，我们一直在讨论为
content.en_text: Over the last few weeks we've been talking about the challenges of producing food for

content.cn_text: 全球人口生产食物所面临的挑战。
content.en_text: the global population.

content.cn_text: 最近，人们对新的食物来源非常感兴趣，其中最
content.en_text: And there's been a lot of interest recently in new sources of food and one of the most

content.cn_text: 有趣的一种就是昆虫。
content.en_text: interesting is insects.

content.cn_text: 那么，为什么昆虫应该被视为一种食物来源呢？
content.en_text: Well, why should insects be considered as a food source?

content.cn_text: 首先，不少昆虫富含蛋白质，而且许多昆虫还含有某些维生素，
content.en_text: Well, for a start a number of them are full of protein and many also have certain vitamins

content.cn_text: 这可能会让你惊讶，事实上比典型的农场动物含量还多。
content.en_text: which may surprise you, more so than a typical farm animal in fact.

content.cn_text: 与粪便中含有大量氨的农场动物相比，昆虫的废弃物
content.en_text: And compared to farm animals whose waste products contain a great deal of ammonia, the waste

content.cn_text: 酸性要低得多，因此几乎不会对
content.en_text: products of insects are far less acidic and would therefore do very little damage to

content.cn_text: 土壤或流经农田的任何河流造成损害。
content.en_text: the soil or to any of the rivers that run through farmland.

content.cn_text: 此外，就昆虫生产而言，成本会非常低。
content.en_text: Furthermore, when it comes to the production of insects the expense would be very low.

content.cn_text: 不需要砍伐树木，也不需要把森林变成牧场，所以它们占用的空间
content.en_text: No trees need to be chopped down or forest turned to pasture so the space that they would

content.cn_text: 会比比如牛或羊少得多。
content.en_text: occupy would be radically smaller than for say cows or sheep.

content.cn_text: 但即使一些消费者决定不愿意把昆虫放进自己的购物
content.en_text: But even if some consumers decided that they'd rather not put insects in their shopping

content.cn_text: 篮里，我们也可以考虑如何把昆虫添加到动物饲料中，也就是给
content.en_text: basket we could also consider how insects might be added to animal feed, the food given

content.cn_text: 农场动物吃的食物。
content.en_text: to farm animals.

content.cn_text: 人们已经知道，如果某些昆虫成为动物饲料的一部分，它们会
content.en_text: It's already known that some insects, if they were part of the animal feed, would act

content.cn_text: 在牛或羊的消化系统中起到一种天然抗生素的作用，从而消除
content.en_text: as a sort of natural antibiotic within a cow or sheep's digestive system, thus removing

content.cn_text: 人为干预的需要。
content.en_text: the need for human intervention.

content.cn_text: 这对农民来说会是一种成本效益高且环保的替代方案。
content.en_text: This would be a cost effective and environmentally friendly alternative for farmers.

content.cn_text: 然而，如果消费者确实认为昆虫是一种潜在的超级食物，是一种养活
content.en_text: However, if consumers do decide that insects are a potential superfood, a way of feeding

content.cn_text: 世界的方式，那么下一个问题就是：我们从哪里获得它们？
content.en_text: the world, then the next question is where do we source them from?

content.cn_text: 也许从野外获取，还是应该在昆虫农场中饲养？
content.en_text: The wild perhaps or should they be bred on insect farms?

content.cn_text: 如果我们从野外获取，一个潜在后果是太多昆虫会被
content.en_text: Well, if we take from the wild one potential consequence of this is that too many will

content.cn_text: 捕捉，进而导致某个物种变得濒危或灭绝。
content.en_text: be taken and in turn a species could become endangered or extinct.

content.cn_text: 另外，对于从野外采集的昆虫，我们无法知道它们
content.en_text: Then for insects which are harvested from the wild we have no way of knowing where they

content.cn_text: 去过哪里，或接触过什么，这可能会对
content.en_text: have been or what they have come in contact with and this could have harmful consequences

content.cn_text: 人类健康造成有害后果。
content.en_text: for human health.

content.cn_text: 想象一下，如果食品生产商或零售商收到的昆虫携带
content.en_text: Imagine the scenario if food producers or retailers were supplied with insects that were carrying

content.cn_text: 细菌，这并不是我们想加入食物链的东西。
content.en_text: bacteria, this is not something we want to add to the food chain.

content.cn_text: 如果昆虫在农场中饲养，我刚才概述的问题就可以避免，而且在
content.en_text: If insects are bred on farms, the problems I've just outlined could be avoided and in

content.cn_text: 许多国家，对昆虫的需求已经在增长，而且增长得很快，
content.en_text: many countries it's already the case that demand for insects is growing, really growing

content.cn_text: 并不是在人们预期的农村地区，而实际上是在城市中。
content.en_text: and it's not in rural areas where you'd expect, but in fact in the cities.

content.cn_text: 例如在泰国，需求确实大幅增长。然而，在一些国家，
content.en_text: In Thailand for example where the demand has really shot up, however, people in countries

content.cn_text: 昆虫传统上就是人们的食物，当地人会告诉你，当昆虫被
content.en_text: where insects are traditionally what people eat, they'll tell you that when insects are

content.cn_text: 密集养殖时，它们的味道就是不一样了。
content.en_text: intensively farmed their taste is simply not the same.

content.cn_text: 具有讽刺意味的是，人们对牛
content.en_text: Ironically this is the same accusation that people have when it comes to the way that cows

content.cn_text: 和羊的饲养方式也有同样的指责。
content.en_text: and sheep are raised.

content.cn_text: 昆虫养殖者也面临一系列挑战，研究人员也很愿意提供帮助。
content.en_text: Insect farmers also face a series of challenges and researchers are keen to assist.

content.cn_text: 首先，在繁殖方面，不同昆虫有不同的要求。
content.en_text: First of all when it comes to breeding different insects have different requirements.

content.cn_text: 首先，对许多物种来说，保持合适的温度以便繁殖是至关重要的。
content.en_text: For a start it's essential to maintain a suitable temperature for many species to reproduce.

content.cn_text: 如果温度不合适，就不会有任何繁殖发生。
content.en_text: If this isn't right nothing's going to happen.

content.cn_text: 另一个问题是，当任何物种在封闭空间中饲养时常常会发生的事情，
content.en_text: Another problem is something that often happens when any species is bred in a confined space

content.cn_text: 到目前为止，研究人员和农民还没有提出任何明确或长期有效的
content.en_text: and so far researchers and farmers have not come up with any clear or long term effective

content.cn_text: 解决办法。
content.en_text: solution to it.

content.cn_text: 我们说的是疾病问题。
content.en_text: We're talking about the problem of disease.

content.cn_text: 你肯定不希望整个昆虫群一次性被消灭。
content.en_text: You don't want a whole colony wiped out in one go.

content.cn_text: 还有昆虫需要被养在哪里的问题。
content.en_text: There's also the question of where insects need to be kept.

content.cn_text: 如果昆虫要作为超级食物来养殖，那么用来饲养它们的箱体必须
content.en_text: If insects are to be bred as a superfood then the tanks used to house them have to be

content.cn_text: 设计得更好，这样清洁所需的时间就会减少。
content.en_text: better designed and this has to happen so that cleaning takes up less time.

content.cn_text: 如果我们谈的是大规模生产，我们不希望这是一项耗时的活动。
content.en_text: If we're talking about mass production we don't want this to be a time consuming activity

content.cn_text: 最后，正如我之前所说，昆虫长期以来一直是一些国家饮食的一部分，
content.en_text: and finally like I said before insects have long been part of the diet in some countries

content.cn_text: 但在其他国家并非如此。
content.en_text: but not so in others.

content.cn_text: 如果农民想鼓励比如英国、法国或德国的人吃昆虫，
content.en_text: The farmers want to encourage people in say England or France or Germany to eat insects

content.cn_text: 那么他们就必须制定一种策略，而我说的不是生产策略，
content.en_text: then they're going to have to develop a strategy and I'm not talking about production

content.cn_text: 而是营销。
content.en_text: but marketing.

content.cn_text: 也就是能说服潜在顾客尝试不同东西的策略。
content.en_text: Something that'll persuade potential customers to try something different.

content.cn_text: 好了，我已经给大家概述了整体情况。
content.en_text: Well, I've given you a general overview of the situation.

content.cn_text: 现在我们来看一些具体例子。
content.en_text: Now let's look at some specific examples.

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31–40

items.title: They have a lot of protein and contain 31. __________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第31题问昆虫含有大量蛋白质并含有什么，原文说 many also have certain vitamins，因此答案是 vitamins。

items.title: Their waste products don’t harm the soil or the 32. __________ on farmland.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第32题问昆虫废弃物不会伤害土壤或农田上的什么，原文说 the soil or any of the rivers，因此答案是 rivers。

items.title: Their production requires less expense and little 33. __________ compared to other animals.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第33题问与其他动物相比，昆虫生产成本低且需要很少的什么，原文说 the space they would occupy would be radically smaller，因此答案是 space。

items.title: Some can be added to animal feed as a kind of 34. __________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第34题问某些昆虫可以作为一种什么加入动物饲料，原文说 act as a sort of natural antibiotic，因此答案是 antibiotic。

items.title: people may be harmed by the 35. __________ they contain.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第35题问野外昆虫可能因含有什么而伤害人类，原文说 insects that were carrying bacteria，因此答案是 bacteria。

items.title: this can supply the growing demand in the 36. __________ of some countries
items.analysis: 答案解析：第36题问昆虫农场可以满足一些国家哪里不断增长的需求，原文说 in fact in the cities，因此答案是 cities。

items.title: the 37. __________ of the insects may be affected.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第37题问昆虫农场养殖会影响昆虫的什么，原文说 their taste is simply not the same，因此答案是 taste。

items.title: Farmers and researchers are unclear how to deal with 38. __________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第38题问农民和研究人员还不清楚如何处理什么，原文说 the problem of disease，因此答案是 disease。

items.title: Better tanks need to be developed so 39. __________ faster.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第39题问需要改进养殖箱，以便什么更快，原文说 cleaning takes up less time，因此答案是 cleaning。

items.title: Farmers need to develop a strategy for 40. __________ the insects.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第40题问农民需要制定什么策略，原文说 not production but marketing，因此答案是 marketing。

groups.source_collect:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
Insects as a food source
Reasons of being a food source
• They have a lot of protein and contain $31$.
• Their waste products don’t harm the soil or the $32$ on farmland.
• Their production requires less expense and little $33$ compared to other animals.
• Some can be added to animal feed as a kind of $34$.
The wild versus farming
From the wild:
• it could lead to the loss of a species
• people may be harmed by the $35$ they contain.
Insect farms:
• this can supply the growing demand in the $36$ of some countries
• the $37$ of the insects may be affected.
The challenges
• Different kinds of insect require a different temperature to breed.
• Farmers and researchers are unclear how to deal with $38$.
• Better tanks need to be developed so $39$ faster.
• Farmers need to develop a strategy for $40$ the insects.
